sync_detail.note: Fresh VM (no pre-existing repo), so the w3_fleetseed INCREMENTAL bundle pattern (prerequisite..head) did not apply directly -- adapted: (1) first tried a shallow (--depth 1) local re-clone + `git bundle create HEAD`, which produced a smaller 89MB bundle but FAILED on the VM (`fatal: Failed to traverse parents ... did not send all necessary objects`) because a shallow clone's bundle omits the parent chain and this git version does not auto-embed shallow-grafting metadata for a bare `git fetch` into an empty repo; abandoned. (2) Fell back to a full-history bundle from the ORIGINAL (non-shallow) Mac working repo: `git branch -f temp-w4-ballgpu-pin 5b268aa6dcf2b5518fb3e7dda235eb8fb89dcad9 && git bundle create w4_code_full.bundle temp-w4-ballgpu-pin` (a bare positive commit SHA is rejected by `git bundle create` with 'Refusing to create empty bundle' -- it needs a real ref, hence the temp branch, deleted immediately after bundling). This full bundle was only 96,763,904 bytes (96MB) -- barely larger than the broken shallow attempt -- and is unconditionally correct (real full history, no missing-parent risk).
sync_detail.transfer_flake_detail: macOS ssh client hit `ssh_packet_write_poll: ... Result too large` (EMSGSIZE) reproducibly (not merely intermittent this run) on plain scp AND plain rsync for every file over roughly 10MB, mid-stream, at unpredictable byte offsets. Workaround: `rsync -a --partial --append-verify --timeout=90 --bwlimit=6000..8000` in a bounded retry loop (append-verify resumes from the last good byte instead of restarting) -- this is the tar_batch + bounded-retries + rsync-fallback pattern named in the spec, applied here as bwlimit-throttled-rsync-with-resume since plain rsync/scp both failed outright, and chunking (`split -b 50m`) was also used for the largest (1.1GB) payload to keep per-attempt blast radius small.
sync_detail.prerequisite: none (full history from repo root, not incremental)

vm.name: pickleball-h100-w4ballgpu
vm.vendor_pin_note: third_party/WASB-SBDT is tracked in the superproject as a bare gitlink (mode 160000, no .gitmodules registered -- per third_party/VENDOR_PINS.md) so the bundle/checkout alone leaves it EMPTY. Its real on-disk content (1.2MB, including any pickleball-local additions layered on top of the pinned upstream sha 923462cacdeb3353b84ddebdedb3f4b7a8553b0f) was tarred directly from the Mac's actual checkout (not re-cloned from GitHub, to guarantee byte-identical local-only additions) and shipped in w4_extra_small.tar, extracted over the empty gitlink directory. `git status --short` on the VM after checkout shows exactly one expected line for this (` ? third_party/WASB-SBDT`, git's gitlink-vs-real-directory marker) -- not a real drift, documented here.

full_tree_proof: remote `git status --short` inside repo/ after checkout is empty except the single expected third_party/WASB-SBDT gitlink marker (explained above) and untracked .venv/ -- i.e. every TRACKED file in the remote checkout byte-matches the 5b268aa6d commit tree, not just the explicit md5 sample below.
